title: Update Posts - Filter by Group/Item and provide "Last Updated" info
excerpt: I would like to be able to Filter Item Updates (in the Update Feed) by Date Entered, by Group, and potentially by Item as well, to be able to see all of the Item and SubItem Update posts for specific Groups.  This should…

posts.author: RLMyrick (Rebecca Myrick)
posts.cookedHtml:
I would like to be able to Filter Item Updates (in the Update Feed) by Date Entered, by Group, and potentially by Item as well, to be able to see all of the Item and SubItem Update posts for specific Groups. This should include all read and unread items, regardless of poster or recipient, for an entire Group, to help assemble comments left by users.

Also, it would be great to be able to see when each Item has last received an Update post, and who the poster was. This would be similar to the “Last Updated” Column, but would provide results for the Update Posts for an Item, and not for “Activity Log” updates to the entries in the Item’s Columns.

posts.author: MCautreels (Maarten Cautreels - Tryve)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Rebecca,

Regarding your first section, this would require extra options on the updates widget or a 3rd party app to be built for this.

But regarding your second section, you can actually achieve this with an automation that looks like this:

image628×299 11.6 KB

This automation will update the “Last Updated Posted” Date column every time a new update is created.

Hope this helps.

Best,

Maarten

posts.author: RLMyrick (Rebecca Myrick)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks for the idea! I have yet to deeply explore the potential automations.

posts.author: david5 (clarks)
posts.cookedHtml: You’re requesting filtering of the Update Feed by date, group, and item, including both read/unread updates, to consolidate group discussions. Additionally, you need a “Last Update Post” field showing the poster and timestamp for each item, distinct from the existing “Last Updated” column which tracks activity log changes. This would facilitate better tracking of user comments and recent activity within specific groups.
